topic.what: Exact fixed-point multiplication over power-of-two rings Z_2^k in honest-majority replicated 3PC
topic.how: Unified quotient-correction framework reducing cross-ring operations to 2-bit bounded-quotient extraction for maliciously secure exact signed truncation and signed extension, composed into end-to-end multiplication protocol
topic.why_matters: Enables privacy-preserving ML with exact semantics and malicious security; first protocol simultaneously achieving cross-ring compatibility, exact output, and malicious security in 3-party replicated setting

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs SIRNN 5252-22928 bits (2-party semi-honest exact); vs Ditto 128-512 bits (3-party semi-honest approx, restricted input)
characteristics.context: Inst1 m=8/n=16/τ=4: 678 bits; Inst2 m=16/n=32/τ=8: 1230 bits; Inst3 m=32/n=64/τ=16: 2334 bits
Inst1 ℓ=16/k=4: 114 bits; Inst2 ℓ=32/k=8: 186 bits; Inst3 ℓ=64/k=16: 330 bits

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs SIRNN <1272-4704 bits (2-party semi-honest); vs Guo <136-320 bits (2-party semi-honest, restricted input)
characteristics.context: Inst1 m=8/n=16: 186 bits; Inst2 m=16/n=32: 330 bits; Inst3 m=32/n=64: 618 bits

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs SIRNN 94.52-1618.12 ms (2-party semi-honest exact); vs Ditto 21.50-201.70 ms (3-party semi-honest approx)
characteristics.context: LAN RTT=10ms and WAN RTT=100ms measured; Ours-mal malicious exact across 3 instantiations
Audited openings require O(1) rounds; Beaver triple multiplication with 2 masked openings

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs Ditto approx (bounded error); vs SIRNN exact but 2-party semi-honest only
characteristics.context: First protocol achieving exact semantics with malicious security in 3-party setting

novelty: Unified quotient-correction framework unifying exact truncation and signed extension via 2-bit bounded-quotient extraction
First end-to-end exact fixed-point multiplication with malicious security in honest-majority 3PC setting
Modular UC proofs via common ideal functionality interface
Optimized 1-ULP error variant with zero online communication for relaxed scenarios
open_problems: Extension to n-party honest-majority beyond 3 parties
Integration with larger preprocessing backends beyond daBits-based implementations
Practical performance optimization for production deployment
Generalization to arbitrary moduli beyond power-of-two rings
